qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Scoped shared token can download arbitrary attachments by file_id
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a missing authorization check in view.php attachment download handling, while the advisory root cause is OS command injection in src/Util/XML.php::decodeP7M() reached from .p7m invoice import paths.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable decodeP7M() command-injection path. The only qualifying finding is a separate scoped-token attachment read in view.php.
evidence: The advisory describes src/Util/XML.php:100, where decodeP7M() passes the attacker-influenced $file path into exec('openssl smime -verify -noverify -in "'.$file.'" -inform DER -out "'.$output_file.'"').

The advisory entrypoints are the invoice import .p7m flows in plugins/importFE_ZIP/actions.php:125-126 and plugins/importFE/src/FatturaElettronica.php:55-56, which route attacker-controlled filenames into XML::decodeP7M().
The only qualifying EvoHunt finding instead targets view.php:23-38, where a shared-token session can request arbitrary file_id values and receive attachment bytes without record-scope enforcement.
That qualifying finding demonstrates cross-record attachment disclosure, not ZIP import, P7M processing, shell metacharacter injection, or command execution.
